mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes M‑Class ML500 / ML550 / ML63 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M113 V8 typically costs £3,500–£6,000 supply only; the M273 costs £4,500–£7,500; the M156 costs £6,000–£10,000; the M278 costs £5,500–£8,500; and the M157 costs £7,000–£11,000. Fitting adds £1,300–£2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your M‑Class.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes M‑Class V8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends which engine your M‑Class has. The M113 is one of Mercedes' most robust engines. The M273 has timing chain stretch issues. The M156 is a legendary AMG V8 but has head bolt issues. The M278 has cylinder scoring problems. The M157 has oil leaks and thermostat issues. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the M‑Class V8 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: M113 (W163): valve stem seal wear. M273 (W164): timing chain stretch. M156 (W164 ML63 AMG): head bolt failure. M278 (W166 ML550): cylinder scoring. M157 (W166 ML63 AMG): oil leaks and thermostat issues. Each engine has its own character and watchpoints.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an M‑Class V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. A W163 ML500 is a £3,000–£8,000 vehicle — a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 50–167% of the car's value; replacement may exceed our 70% Mercedes scrap threshold. A W164 ML63 AMG is a £10,000–£25,000 vehicle — a £7,000–£8,000 recon is 28–80% of the car's value. A W166 ML63 AMG is a £15,000–£35,000 vehicle — a £8,000–£9,000 recon is 23–60% of the car's value. Evaluate carefully based on your car's generation and condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes M‑Class engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The ML500 uses M113 (W163) or M273 (W164). The ML550 uses M273 (W164) or M278 (W166). The ML63 AMG uses M156 (W164) or M157 (W166). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on an M‑Class V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: On M113: listen for blue smoke on startup — valve stem seal indicator. On M273: timing chain replacement at 80,000–100,000 miles is recommended. On M156: have the head bolts replaced with upgraded units — this is the single most important investment you can make. On M278: listen for piston slap — consider bore‑scoping. On M157: regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved oil and shortened intervals (5,000–7,500 miles) are essential. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, blue smoke, coolant loss or piston slap, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes M‑Class V8 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the range across all generations; specific costs vary by era — see the Era Map above. A replacement M157 engine for the ML63 AMG can cost in excess of £10,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an M‑Class V8 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). V8 engines require significant disassembly; some turbo replacements on the M157 require the engine to be removed.

cta.label: → Compare M‑Class V8 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Valve Stem Seal Wear (M113 — W163 ML500, 2002–2005)
problems.symptoms: Blue smoke on startup (particularly after idling), excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles+), fouled spark plugs.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£4,000 (valve stem seal replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The M113's valve stem seals harden over time, allowing oil to leak into the combustion chamber on startup. If you see blue smoke on startup or notice increasing oil consumption, have the valve stem seals inspected. Replacing them is labour‑intensive but far cheaper than a full engine replacement.

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (M273 — W164 ML500/ML550, 2006–2011)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start (2–5 seconds), metallic ticking, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (chain, guides and tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M273's timing chain is a known weak point. The chain stretches over time, producing a distinct rattle on cold start. If left unaddressed, the chain can jump or snap, resulting in catastrophic engine damage. Regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved oil are essential. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, have the timing chain system inspected immediately.

problems.title: Head Bolt Failure (M156 — W164 ML63 AMG, 2006–2011)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, white smoke from exhaust, rough idle, engine warning light, external coolant leaks.
problems.repairCost: £2,000–£5,000 (head bolt replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £6,000–£10,000 (full engine replacement if head lifts and causes extensive damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you notice coolant loss or overheating, stop driving and book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M156's head bolts are the most significant weakness — the bolts can stretch or break, allowing the cylinder head to lift and coolant to leak. If left unaddressed, the engine can suffer catastrophic failure. Mercedes‑Benz issued a technical service bulletin (LI05.20‑P‑050856) for this issue. If you own an M156 ML63 AMG, checking for head bolt issues is essential. Upgraded head bolts are available as a permanent fix.

problems.title: Cylinder Scoring (M278 — W166 ML550, 2012–2015)
problems.symptoms: Piston slap on startup, loss of power, increased oil consumption, metallic knocking, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £4,000–£8,000 (engine rebuild or cylinder bore remediation)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£8,500 (full engine replacement if scoring is extensive)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear piston slap or knocking, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M278 twin‑turbo V8 has a well‑documented cylinder scoring issue. Deep scratches can appear in the cylinder walls, causing increased oil consumption and eventual engine failure. A bore‑scope inspection is the only way to confirm cylinder condition. If buying a W166 ML550, listen carefully for piston slap on startup and consider having the cylinders bore‑scoped.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: M‑Class V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — before chain snaps
Often requires full rebuild; assess overall engine health
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This M‑Class V8? Check These First
buyIf: * M113 (W163): no blue smoke on startup — valve stem seals in good condition
* M273 (W164): no cold‑start rattle — timing chain in good condition
* M156 (W164 ML63): head bolts replaced with upgraded units
* M278 (W166 ML550): no piston slap — cylinders bore‑scoped and clear
* Full Mercedes service history with regular oil changes
avoidIf: * M113: blue smoke on startup — valve stem seals may be failing
* M273: cold‑start rattle with no chain history — walk away
* M156: coolant loss with no diagnosis — head bolts may be failing — walk away
* M278: piston slap on startup — cylinder scoring likely — walk away

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M‑Class V8 badges cover five distinct Mercedes‑Benz and AMG engines across three generations. The W163's M113 is the most robust, the W164's M273 and M156 have timing chain and head bolt weaknesses, and the W166's M278 and M157 have cylinder scoring and oil leak issues. Buy on full service history — and never ignore a cold‑start rattle, blue smoke or coolant loss.

cta.label: → Compare M‑Class V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes M‑Class ML500 / ML550 / ML63 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (M273 timing chain) or piston slap (M278 cylinder scoring)
* Blue Smoke — Check on startup (M113 valve stem seals)
* Coolant Level — Check for unexplained loss (M156 head bolts)
* Service History — including which engine and confirmed oil‑change intervals
* Head Bolt History (M156) — Check if upgraded bolts installed
* Cylinder Scoring (M278) — Listen for piston slap; consider bore‑scoping
* Oil Leaks (M157) — Check under the car and around the engine
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly. M113 engine codes include M113.964 and M113.965. M156 engine code is M156.980.
